title: Cyrtochilum pastorellii
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum pastorellii is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the Americas. Cyrtochilum pastorellii is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. This classification is further divided into the tribe Cylindrieae. Cyrtochilum pastorellii is also a member of the genus Cyrtochilum, which consists of approximately 500 species of orchids.

intro: Cyrtochilum pastorellii, also known as Pastorelli's Cyrtochilum, is a species of orchid native to Peru. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and digestive issues. Cyrtochilum pastorellii contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cyrtochilum pastorellii is used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
